title: Tesla’s robotaxis are moving in reverse
excerpt: The number of paid robotaxi miles traveled fell 36% in the second quarter, despite expanding to new cities, according to Tesla's own figures.
content_html:
Tesla’s budding “Robotaxi” network drove fewer miles for paying customers in the second quarter than it did in the first, according to a chart the company released on Wednesday.

The quarter-over-quarter decline runs counter to Tesla’s rhetoric and actions in the past year. Tesla has staked much of its future on the idea of a massive, low-cost, cash-generating Robotaxi fleet — or going “balls to the wall for autonomy,” as CEO Elon Musk framed it in 2024. The quarterly step-down in Robotaxi miles also comes amid weakening profits in Tesla’s core businesses, which underperformed Wall Street’s expectations, according to figures released Wednesday. Tesla’s stock plunged more than 13% in early trading on Thursday.

At a passing glance, the chart appears to show steady growth in paid Robotaxi rides between August 2025 and June 2026. But the numbers displayed are cumulative, and when broken down by quarter, they show that Tesla’s Robotaxi fleet of Model Y SUVs carrying paying passengers covered around 1.1 million miles in the first quarter. That fell to roughly 700,000 miles in the second quarter, a decline of about 36%.

That’s despite the fact that the company has expanded its nascent operation to six cities across Texas and Florida, with a mix of unsupervised and supervised vehicles.

It’s likely Tesla is counting the paid miles driven in the San Francisco Bay Area, too, even though these branded Robotaxis don’t have the state-required permits to operate autonomously and also have a safety driver behind the wheel. Tesla has referred to that operation as part of its “Robotaxi coverage.”

The decline in miles driven also comes as Tesla made a striking admission on a conference call Wednesday about its second-quarter results. In response to a question about how slowly Tesla is scaling the Robotaxi service, Musk said the company needs to “accumulate driving data that is specific to the Cybercab” — the company’s gold, purpose-built, two-seater sedan that is expected to make up the bulk of its autonomous vehicle fleet — “before we can put a lot of them on the road.”

“Unlike, say, Model 3, Model Y, and our other vehicles where we’ve got a lot of vehicles on the road, millions of vehicles on the road, we don’t have that for Cybercab. So we actually have to accumulate miles with Cybercabs that are retrofitted with steering wheels and acceleration and braking pedals, that kind of thing, to calibrate to the Cybercab chassis,” he said. “As we are confident about that, the number of Cybercabs in cities will increase dramatically.”

This represents something of a break from claims the company has made for years about how its fleet of nearly 10 million customer cars has been silently collecting data in the background to train future robotaxis (in addition to training the driver assistance software for consumers, which Tesla calls Full Self-Driving).

On the call, Tesla executives framed the slow progress as a matter of being cautious about safety.

“Our goals are very ambitious for Robotaxi, but we do need to be cautious about causing any accidents or causing any harm to anyone,” Musk said.

He then said he doesn’t want Tesla Robotaxis causing accidents because he thinks bad media coverage could lead to a regulatory crackdown.

“Although there are, I think, 30 to 40,000 automotive deaths per year in the United States alone, most of those do not generate any press, you don’t really read about almost any of those. But if we injure even one person, it will be worldwide headline news, and regulators will immediately clamp down on our activities,” he said.

Ashok Elluswamy, Tesla’s VP of AI, boasted that Tesla’s Robotaxis have had “zero notable incidents” while driving “more than 380,000 miles” without a safety operator onboard. He did not define what the company considers “notable incidents,” though he claimed that “any reports have been of other actors impacting us when we were stationary.”

Tesla has reported 22 crashes to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration in the year since it started trialing its Robotaxi service. While most of them involve other cars crashing into Tesla’s Robotaxis, the company has reported three crashes caused by its teleoperators moving the vehicles remotely, and multiple instances of the cars hitting objects at low speeds including curbs, utility poles, and a tow truck’s bed.

This represents another narrative change for the company. Tesla spent years claiming that the largest hurdle to full-scale Robotaxi deployment was regulatory in nature — though the company never really specified what those prohibitive regulations were.

Now the company says proving safety is all that’s holding Robotaxis back. And although it is still in the very early stages, Tesla still chose this moment to take a victory lap about its decision to build an autonomy stack that doesn’t use radar or lidar sensors, like industry leader Waymo.

“Historically, the so-called experts have always claimed that you need lidars, radars, HD maps, and the entire kitchen sink to drive safely. Here, we show that such is not true. You can have safe, comfortable, and affordable autonomy with just cameras,” Elluswamy said.

Both Musk and Elluswamy also promised growth is coming. They noted that the number of unsupervised miles traveled has grown roughly 10% every week since Tesla started offering them at the end of last year.

“We’ll continue to scale, I think, very, very rapidly,” Musk said.

When you purchase through links in our articles, we may earn a small commission. This doesn’t affect our editorial independence.

sentences.en: Tesla’s budding “Robotaxi” network drove fewer miles for paying customers in the second quarter than it did in the first, according to a chart the company released on Wednesday.
sentences.zh: 根据特斯拉周三发布的图表，特斯拉新兴的“Robotaxi”网络在第二季度为付费客户提供的行驶里程少于第一季度。

blocks.html_en: The quarter-over-quarter decline runs counter to Tesla’s rhetoric and actions in the past year. Tesla has staked much of its future on the idea of a massive, low-cost, cash-generating Robotaxi fleet — or going “balls to the wall for autonomy,” as CEO Elon Musk framed it in 2024. The quarterly step-down in Robotaxi miles also comes amid weakening profits in Tesla’s core businesses, which underperformed Wall Street’s expectations, according to figures released Wednesday. Tesla’s stock plunged more than 13% in early trading on Thursday.

sentences.en: The quarter-over-quarter decline runs counter to Tesla’s rhetoric and actions in the past year.
sentences.zh: 这一季度环比下降与特斯拉过去一年的言论和行动背道而驰。

sentences.en: Tesla has staked much of its future on the idea of a massive, low-cost, cash-generating Robotaxi fleet — or going “balls to the wall for autonomy,” as CEO Elon Musk framed it in 2024.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉将其未来的大部分赌注押在了大规模、低成本、能产生现金的 Robotaxi 车队的想法上，或者正如首席执行官埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 在 2024 年所描述的那样，“为实现自动驾驶而拼尽全力”。

sentences.en: The quarterly step-down in Robotaxi miles also comes amid weakening profits in Tesla’s core businesses, which underperformed Wall Street’s expectations, according to figures released Wednesday.
sentences.zh: 根据周三公布的数据，特斯拉核心业务利润疲软，其表现不及华尔街预期，Robotaxi 里程数季度下降也是如此。

sentences.en: Tesla’s stock plunged more than 13% in early trading on Thursday.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉股价周四早盘暴跌逾 13%。

blocks.html_en: At a passing glance, the chart appears to show steady growth in paid Robotaxi rides between August 2025 and June 2026. But the numbers displayed are cumulative, and when broken down by quarter, they show that Tesla’s Robotaxi fleet of Model Y SUVs carrying paying passengers covered around 1.1 million miles in the first quarter. That fell to roughly 700,000 miles in the second quarter, a decline of about 36%.

sentences.en: At a passing glance, the chart appears to show steady growth in paid Robotaxi rides between August 2025 and June 2026.
sentences.zh: 乍一看，该图表似乎显示 2025 年 8 月至 2026 年 6 月期间，付费 Robotaxi 乘车量稳步增长。

sentences.en: But the numbers displayed are cumulative, and when broken down by quarter, they show that Tesla’s Robotaxi fleet of Model Y SUVs carrying paying passengers covered around 1.1 million miles in the first quarter.
sentences.zh: 但显示的数字是累积的，按季度细分后，显示特斯拉的 Robotaxi 车队的 Model Y SUV 载着付费乘客在第一季度行驶了约 110 万英里。

sentences.en: That fell to roughly 700,000 miles in the second quarter, a decline of about 36%.
sentences.zh: 第二季度这一数字下降至约 70 万英里，降幅约为 36%。

blocks.html_en: That’s despite the fact that the company has expanded its nascent operation to six cities across Texas and Florida, with a mix of unsupervised and supervised vehicles.

sentences.en: That’s despite the fact that the company has expanded its nascent operation to six cities across Texas and Florida, with a mix of unsupervised and supervised vehicles.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，该公司已将其新生业务扩展到德克萨斯州和佛罗里达州的六个城市，并混合使用无人监管和监管车辆。

sentences.en: Image Credits:Tesla
sentences.zh: 图片来源：特斯拉

sentences.en: It’s likely Tesla is counting the paid miles driven in the San Francisco Bay Area, too, even though these branded Robotaxis don’t have the state-required permits to operate autonomously and also have a safety driver behind the wheel.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉很可能也在计算在旧金山湾区的付费行驶里程，尽管这些品牌的机器人出租车没有国家要求的自动驾驶许可证，也没有安全驾驶员。

sentences.en: Tesla has referred to that operation as part of its “Robotaxi coverage.”
sentences.zh: 特斯拉将该操作称为其“Robotaxi 覆盖范围”的一部分。

sentences.en: The decline in miles driven also comes as Tesla made a striking admission on a conference call Wednesday about its second-quarter results.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉在周三的电话会议上对其第二季度业绩做出了惊人的承认，驾驶里程也出现了下降。

sentences.en: In response to a question about how slowly Tesla is scaling the Robotaxi service, Musk said the company needs to “accumulate driving data that is specific to the Cybercab” — the company’s gold, purpose-built, two-seater sedan that is expected to make up the bulk of its autonomous vehicle fleet — “before we can put a lot of them on the road.”
sentences.zh: 在回答有关特斯拉扩展 Robotaxi 服务的速度有多慢的问题时，马斯克表示，该公司需要“积累 Cyber​​cab 特有的驾驶数据”——该公司的黄金专用两座轿车，预计将构成其自动驾驶车队的大部分——“然后我们才能将它们大量上路。”

blocks.html_en: “Unlike, say, Model 3, Model Y, and our other vehicles where we’ve got a lot of vehicles on the road, millions of vehicles on the road, we don’t have that for Cybercab. So we actually have to accumulate miles with Cybercabs that are retrofitted with steering wheels and acceleration and braking pedals, that kind of thing, to calibrate to the Cybercab chassis,” he said. “As we are confident about that, the number of Cybercabs in cities will increase dramatically.”

sentences.zh: “与 Model 3、Model Y 和我们的其他车辆不同，我们在路上有很多车辆，数百万辆车辆在路上，但我们没有 Cyber​​cab。

sentences.en: So we actually have to accumulate miles with Cybercabs that are retrofitted with steering wheels and acceleration and braking pedals, that kind of thing, to calibrate to the Cybercab chassis,” he said.
sentences.zh: 因此，我们实际上必须使用改装了方向盘、加速踏板和制动踏板之类的 Cyber​​cab 来积累里程，以校准 Cyber​​cab 底盘。”他说。

sentences.en: “As we are confident about that, the number of Cybercabs in cities will increase dramatically.”
sentences.zh: “我们对此充满信心，城市中网约车的数量将大幅增加。”

blocks.html_en: This represents something of a break from claims the company has made for years about how its fleet of nearly 10 million customer cars has been silently collecting data in the background to train future robotaxis (in addition to training the driver assistance software for consumers, which Tesla calls Full Self-Driving).

sentences.en: This represents something of a break from claims the company has made for years about how its fleet of nearly 10 million customer cars has been silently collecting data in the background to train future robotaxis (in addition to training the driver assistance software for consumers, which Tesla calls Full Self-Driving).

blocks.html_en: On the call, Tesla executives framed the slow progress as a matter of being cautious about safety.

blocks.html_en: “Our goals are very ambitious for Robotaxi, but we do need to be cautious about causing any accidents or causing any harm to anyone,” Musk said.

sentences.en: “Our goals are very ambitious for Robotaxi, but we do need to be cautious about causing any accidents or causing any harm to anyone,” Musk said.
sentences.zh: 马斯克表示：“我们对 Robotaxi 的目标非常雄心勃勃，但我们确实需要谨慎行事，避免造成任何事故或对任何人造成任何伤害。”

blocks.html_en: “Although there are, I think, 30 to 40,000 automotive deaths per year in the United States alone, most of those do not generate any press, you don’t really read about almost any of those. But if we injure even one person, it will be worldwide headline news, and regulators will immediately clamp down on our activities,” he said.

sentences.en: “Although there are, I think, 30 to 40,000 automotive deaths per year in the United States alone, most of those do not generate any press, you don’t really read about almost any of those.
sentences.zh: “我认为，尽管仅在美国每年就有 30 至 40,000 人因车祸死亡，但其中大多数并没有引起任何媒体关注，你几乎不会真正读到其中任何一篇。

sentences.en: But if we injure even one person, it will be worldwide headline news, and regulators will immediately clamp down on our activities,” he said.
sentences.zh: 但如果我们伤害哪怕一个人，这都会成为全球头条新闻，监管机构将立即取缔我们的活动，”他说。

sentences.zh: 特斯拉人工智能副总裁 Ashok Elluswamy 吹嘘说，特斯拉的 Robotaxis 在没有安全操作员的情况下行驶“超过 380,000 英里”时“零发生过重大事故”。

sentences.en: He did not define what the company considers “notable incidents,” though he claimed that “any reports have been of other actors impacting us when we were stationary.”
sentences.zh: 他没有定义公司认为的“值得注意的事件”，尽管他声称“任何报告都表明其他行为者在我们静止不动时影响了我们。”

sentences.en: While most of them involve other cars crashing into Tesla’s Robotaxis, the company has reported three crashes caused by its teleoperators moving the vehicles remotely, and multiple instances of the cars hitting objects at low speeds including curbs, utility poles, and a tow truck’s bed.

blocks.html_en: “Historically, the so-called experts have always claimed that you need lidars, radars, HD maps, and the entire kitchen sink to drive safely. Here, we show that such is not true. You can have safe, comfortable, and affordable autonomy with just cameras,” Elluswamy said.

sentences.en: Here, we show that such is not true.
sentences.zh: 在这里，我们表明事实并非如此。

sentences.en: You can have safe, comfortable, and affordable autonomy with just cameras,” Elluswamy said.
sentences.zh: 只需摄像头，你就可以拥有安全、舒适且价格实惠的自主权。”Elluswamy 说道。

sentences.zh: 这并不影响我们的编辑独立性。

content_html_zh:
根据特斯拉周三发布的图表，特斯拉新兴的“Robotaxi”网络在第二季度为付费客户提供的行驶里程少于第一季度。

这一季度环比下降与特斯拉过去一年的言论和行动背道而驰。 特斯拉将其未来的大部分赌注押在了大规模、低成本、能产生现金的 Robotaxi 车队的想法上，或者正如首席执行官埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 在 2024 年所描述的那样，“为实现自动驾驶而拼尽全力”。 根据周三公布的数据，特斯拉核心业务利润疲软，其表现不及华尔街预期，Robotaxi 里程数季度下降也是如此。 特斯拉股价周四早盘暴跌逾 13%。

乍一看，该图表似乎显示 2025 年 8 月至 2026 年 6 月期间，付费 Robotaxi 乘车量稳步增长。 但显示的数字是累积的，按季度细分后，显示特斯拉的 Robotaxi 车队的 Model Y SUV 载着付费乘客在第一季度行驶了约 110 万英里。 第二季度这一数字下降至约 70 万英里，降幅约为 36%。

尽管如此，该公司已将其新生业务扩展到德克萨斯州和佛罗里达州的六个城市，并混合使用无人监管和监管车辆。

特斯拉很可能也在计算在旧金山湾区的付费行驶里程，尽管这些品牌的机器人出租车没有国家要求的自动驾驶许可证，也没有安全驾驶员。 特斯拉将该操作称为其“Robotaxi 覆盖范围”的一部分。

特斯拉在周三的电话会议上对其第二季度业绩做出了惊人的承认，驾驶里程也出现了下降。 在回答有关特斯拉扩展 Robotaxi 服务的速度有多慢的问题时，马斯克表示，该公司需要“积累 Cyber​​cab 特有的驾驶数据”——该公司的黄金专用两座轿车，预计将构成其自动驾驶车队的大部分——“然后我们才能将它们大量上路。”

“与 Model 3、Model Y 和我们的其他车辆不同，我们在路上有很多车辆，数百万辆车辆在路上，但我们没有 Cyber​​cab。 因此，我们实际上必须使用改装了方向盘、加速踏板和制动踏板之类的 Cyber​​cab 来积累里程，以校准 Cyber​​cab 底盘。”他说。 “我们对此充满信心，城市中网约车的数量将大幅增加。”

这在一定程度上打破了该公司多年来声称的其近 1000 万辆客户汽车车队如何在后台默默收集数据以训练未来的机器人出租车（此外还为消费者培训驾驶员辅助软件，特斯拉称之为完全自动驾驶）的说法。

在电话会议上，特斯拉高管将进展缓慢归咎于对安全的谨慎态度。

马斯克表示：“我们对 Robotaxi 的目标非常雄心勃勃，但我们确实需要谨慎行事，避免造成任何事故或对任何人造成任何伤害。”

然后他表示，他不希望特斯拉机器人出租车引发事故，因为他认为不良媒体报道可能会导致监管部门的打击。

“我认为，尽管仅在美国每年就有 30 至 40,000 人因车祸死亡，但其中大多数并没有引起任何媒体关注，你几乎不会真正读到其中任何一篇。 但如果我们伤害哪怕一个人，这都会成为全球头条新闻，监管机构将立即取缔我们的活动，”他说。

特斯拉人工智能副总裁 Ashok Elluswamy 吹嘘说，特斯拉的 Robotaxis 在没有安全操作员的情况下行驶“超过 380,000 英里”时“零发生过重大事故”。 他没有定义公司认为的“值得注意的事件”，尽管他声称“任何报告都表明其他行为者在我们静止不动时影响了我们。”

自开始试用其 Robotaxi 服务以来，特斯拉今年已向美国国家公路交通安全管理局报告了 22 起事故。 虽然其中大多数涉及其他汽车撞上特斯拉的 Robotaxis，但该公司报告了三起由远程操作员远程移动车辆造成的撞车事故，以及多起汽车低速撞上物体的情况，包括路缘石、电线杆和拖车车底。

这代表着该公司的另一个叙述性变化。 特斯拉多年来一直声称，全面部署 Robotaxi 的最大障碍本质上是监管——尽管该公司从未真正具体说明这些禁止性规定是什么。

现在该公司表示，安全性是阻碍 Robotaxis 发展的唯一因素。 尽管仍处于非常早期的阶段，特斯拉仍然选择了这个时刻来庆祝其决定建立一个不使用雷达或激光雷达传感器的自动驾驶堆栈，就像行业领导者 Waymo 一样。

“从历史上看，所谓的专家总是声称，你需要激光雷达、雷达、高清地图和整个厨房水槽才能安全驾驶。 在这里，我们表明事实并非如此。 只需摄像头，你就可以拥有安全、舒适且价格实惠的自主权。”Elluswamy 说道。

马斯克和埃勒斯瓦米都承诺增长即将到来。 他们指出，自特斯拉去年底开始提供无人监管服务以来，无人监管行驶里程数每周增长约 10%。

“我认为，我们将继续非常非常迅速地扩大规模，”马斯克说。

当您通过我们文章中的链接购买时，我们可能会赚取少量佣金。 这并不影响我们的编辑独立性。
